articles.title: 108yo and 103yo WWII veterans meet after more than 80 years

children.content: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

image.altText: Two elderly men seated at a table set for a lunch with a paper program

articles.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith speaks after Anzac Day event in Queensland

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith shook hands and posed for photos with fellow attendees after the service on the Gold Coast wrapped up.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith shakes hands with supporters in a crowd on a rainy day.

children.content: Australians, including Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Opposition Leader Angus Taylor, gather to commemorate Anzac Day at dawn services this morning. Follow live.

items.title: Australians come together to mark Anzac Day

updates.title: Check in on veterans who may be alone today, RSL SA chair says
'Ugly behaviour': Victoria premier condemns booing during Dawn Service

items.title: ICAC investigating senior SA police officer for alleged misconduct

children.content: The state's anti-corruption agency says the officer, who cannot be named, is under investigation to determine whether they "committed a breach of discipline contrary to the SA Police Code of Conduct".

image.altText: A building with a piping shrike logo on it

items.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care

children.content: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

image.altText: A woman with blue eyes and short blonde hair smiles. She is outside.

items.title: Bail denied for woman accused of repeatedly trying to kill ex-partner

children.content: The accused's lawyers applied for her release on bail last year on the basis that she had "dental and medical issues" that could not be managed in prison.

image.altText: A woman sitting at a desk in front of a bookcase

items.title: Oyster growers enduring 'death by a thousand cuts' after being closed again

children.content: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

image.altText: A man standing waist deep in water

items.title: New Adelaide Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

gallery.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

thumbnail.altText: A smiling woman leans an arm over a park bench

items.title: Councillor convicted of drink driving still able to attend Anzac Day parade

children.content: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

image.altText: a man smiles at the camera

items.title: Law firm ordered to pay more than $4.7m after overcharging outback council

children.content: The NSW Supreme Court has ordered a Sydney-based law firm and related companies to pay the Broken Hill City Council more than $4.7 million.

image.altText: picture of a sign saying 'supreme court'

items.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing

children.content: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

image.altText: Melbourne Demons' Kysaiah Pickett jumps into a Richmond player to take a mark in an AFL game.

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

gallery.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

thumbnail.altText: A figure skater wearing skates on ice holders her hands in the air

items.title: 'Unusual' warm weather sees Adelaide residents spending days at beach

children.content: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

items.title: The chart that shows how much the NDIS price tag has blown out

children.content: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

image.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: The 'internationally acclaimed' French sculptor who is nowhere to be found

children.content: An Australian gallery has been selling sculptures as the work of an "internationally acclaimed" French artist. But the Paris backstory doesn’t stack up.

gallery.altText: A woman with short greying hair holds a crumbled blue sheet of plastic upright.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with short greying hair holds a crumbled blue sheet of plastic upright.

items.title: One Nation's outcome in Farrer could be a watershed moment

children.content: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

image.altText: David Farley speaks while reclining on a park bench.

items.title: James Valentine knew good radio and I was his wide-eyed apprentice

children.content: As an aspiring broadcaster, I covertly watched everything James Valentine did on air. He taught me how in the absurd minutia of our lives, we reveal ourselves to each other.

image.altText: a man behind a microphone looks into the camera

items.title: Virginia Giuffre's legacy and the year that followed the Epstein files release

children.content: Virginia Giuffre didn't live to witness the full impact of the truths she brought forward, but the arrests, resignations and the unravelling of reputations came in the wake of her decision to speak.

items.title: Leaked Pentagon email shows US wants to punish NATO allies over Iran war

children.content: The email suggests the US may review the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands and Spain's NATO membership as punishment over their stance on the Iran war.

items.title: Inmates accused of sexual assault in prison continued to share cells, report finds

children.content: A Queensland Ombudsman report has found three instances where prison inmates accused of sexually assaulting cellmates have continued to share cells with other prisoners.

image.altText: A corridor inside a jail with locked doors.

items.title: How Paul Dempsey became Australia's 'king of covers'

children.content: From Taylor Swift to Cher, Paul Dempsey's cover versions have been lauded. But does he have a secret weapon to making the perfect cover?

items.title: 'Things could get ugly': CFMEU interference under spotlight at inquiry

children.content: The commission of inquiry into the CFMEU has this week heard the union had a complex relationship with the former Labor government.

items.title: Quiz: Test yourself on The Shawshank Redemption, hair metal and Bugs Bunny

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Vic govt announces $860m for 7,000 extra social housing properties

children.content: An extra 7,000 social housing properties will be built in Victoria in the coming decade, with an initial $860 million investment in the state budget, the Allan government has announced.
